message:
perf(btree,vdbe): reuse seek-result cursor position for INSERT and IdxInsert (USESEEKRESULT pattern)

When the VDBE probes for an existing rowid before INSERT (via
table_move_to or index_move_to), the cursor lands on the successor
key or at EOF — exactly the position where the new row belongs.
Previously, both table_insert() and index_insert() performed a
redundant full B-tree seek to find this same position.

This commit adds "prechecked absent" insert paths that reuse the
probe's cursor state, eliminating the second seek:

cursor.rs — new methods:
- index_insert_from_current_position(): inserts an index key at the
  cursor's current leaf position (cell_idx or EOF), handling overflow
  chains and balance-for-insert fallback with proper cleanup
- index_insert_prechecked_absent(): public wrapper with btree-op guard,
  mirrors table_insert_prechecked_absent() for index cursors
- index_insert_unique_check_then_insert(): after the uniqueness
  conflict check (which positions the cursor via index_seek), now
  restores the at_eof flag and advances to next() to set up the
  successor-position, then calls index_insert_prechecked_absent()
  when the cursor is on a valid leaf position

engine.rs — VDBE changes:
- table_insert_prechecked_absent() dispatch on CursorBackend (Mem, Txn)
- Insert opcode: captures SeekResult from the existence probe via
  insert_seek_result; when NotFound, calls table_insert_prechecked_absent
  instead of table_insert, skipping the redundant table_move_to
- IdxInsert unique-check path: after the conflict check positions the
  cursor, reuses position for the insert via the cursor's internal
  unique_check_then_insert flow

New tests:
- test_table_insert_prechecked_absent_reuses_eof_position: 4-row leaf,
  insert at rowid=99 (past end), verify sorted order
- test_index_insert_prechecked_absent_reuses_successor_position: 2-key
  index, insert between them via seek+prechecked, verify all 3 in order
- test_index_insert_prechecked_absent_at_eof_appends: insert at EOF
- test_index_insert_unique_check_reuses_seek_position: full
  unique-check flow with 3 prefixes, verifies duplicate prefix detection
  AND non-conflicting insert both work correctly
- test_insert_after_missing_probe_disturbed_by_reposition_stays_sorted:
  VDBE test where SeekRowid disturbs cursor between probe and insert,
  proving the fallback to full table_insert still produces correct order
- test_idxinsert_unique_no_conflict_inserts_distinct_prefix_after_cursor_disturbance:
  VDBE IdxInsert with Found-based cursor disturbance before insert

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
